title: Jensen Huang made $172 billion selling AI chips. He just gave $75 million to an art school.
excerpt: Jensen and Lori Huang have given Vanderbilt University $75 million to establish the Jen-Hsun and Lori Huang College of Art, Architecture and Design in San Francisco. The $75 million is a matching gift, and it has already spurred over $100 million in additional private support from alumni and donors including leaders in the Bay Area […]
content_html:
TL;DR

Jensen and Lori Huang donated $75M to Vanderbilt University for a new art, architecture and design college in San Francisco. It is Huang’s largest university gift. Opens 2027.

Jensen and Lori Huang have given Vanderbilt University $75 million to establish the Jen-Hsun and Lori Huang College of Art, Architecture and Design in San Francisco. The $75 million is a matching gift, and it has already spurred over $100 million in additional private support from alumni and donors including leaders in the Bay Area arts community. It is Huang’s largest gift to any university, surpassing the more than $50 million he has given to his alma mater Oregon State. Both Jensen and Lori Huang are engineers.

The college will open in 2027 at the former California College of the Arts campus in Potrero Hill, building on CCA’s nearly 120-year legacy. Vanderbilt will establish a California College of the Arts Institute to preserve CCA’s traditions. The campus expects to serve about 1,000 undergraduate and graduate students with a focus on engineering, entrepreneurship, and design. A national search is underway for the founding dean. The San Francisco campus is part of Vanderbilt’s broader expansion that includes a New York City campus opening to undergraduates this autumn and a graduate campus planned in West Palm Beach.

The gift is a statement about where the person who built the infrastructure of the AI boom thinks the value actually lies. Google used Veo and Gemini to reconstruct a lost Pelé goal, demonstrating that AI’s most culturally significant applications require artistic judgement, not just compute. Huang has said publicly that AI will create more demand for creative skills, not less. Funding an art school with chip money is the most direct way to make that argument.

Nvidia’s market cap sat at $4.86 trillion at its recent peak. Huang’s signed leather jacket sold for $960,000 at Sotheby’s last week, 96 times its retail price, turning a CEO’s wardrobe into a collectible. The art school donation is a different kind of investment in culture: $75 million to ensure that the generation trained on AI tools also learns what to do with them. Whether a college named after a chip executive can credibly teach that is the question Vanderbilt will need to answer when the doors open.

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

Jensen 和 Lori Huang 向范德比尔特大学捐赠了 7500 万美元，用于在旧金山建立一所新的艺术、建筑和设计学院。 这是黄先生收到的最大的大学礼物。 2027 年开业。

Jensen 和 Lori Huang 向范德比尔特大学捐赠 7500 万美元，用于在旧金山建立 Jen-Hsun 和 Lori Huang 艺术、建筑与设计学院。 7500 万美元是一份匹配的礼物，它已经激发了校友和捐助者（包括湾区艺术界领导人）提供超过 1 亿美元的额外私人支持。 这是黄仁勋向大学捐赠的最大一笔礼物，超过了他向母校俄勒冈州立大学捐赠的超过 5000 万美元。 Jensen 和 Lori Huang 都是工程师。

该学院将于 2027 年在波特雷罗山的前加州艺术学院校园内开业，以 CCA 近 120 年的传统为基础。 范德比尔特大学将建立加州艺术学院学院以保留 CCA 的传统。 该校园预计将为约 1,000 名本科生和研究生提供服务，重点关注工程、创业和设计领域。 全国范围内正在寻找创始院长。 旧金山校区是范德堡大学更广泛扩张的一部分，其中包括今年秋天向本科生开放的纽约市校区以及计划在西棕榈滩开设的研究生校区。

这份礼物表达了人工智能热潮的基础设施建设者认为其真正价值所在的声明。 谷歌使用 Veo 和 Gemini 重建了丢失的贝利进球，证明人工智能最具文化意义的应用需要艺术判断，而不仅仅是计算。 黄仁勋曾公开表示，人工智能将创造更多而不是更少的创意技能需求。 用筹码资助一所艺术学校是表达这一论点的最直接的方式。

Nvidia 的市值达到近期峰值 4.86 万亿美元。 黄的签名皮夹克上周在苏富比拍卖行以 96 万美元的价格售出，是零售价的 96 倍，将首席执行官的衣柜变成了收藏品。 艺术学校的捐赠是对文化的另一种投资：7500万美元用于确保接受过人工智能工具培训的一代人也能学会如何使用它们。 一所以芯片高管命名的大学能否可靠地教授这门课程，这是范德比尔特大学在大门打开时需要回答的问题。
